rulings.Case Title: WATERVIEW DEVELOPMENT LLC VS. VINCENT T.C. TAI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel Civil
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 8, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF WATERVIEW DEVELOPMENT LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel Civil is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Counsel to prepare order. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RODRIGO LOPEZ VS. ALTAS PROPERTY GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 8, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT ATLAS PROPERTY GROUP, INC., 1830 ALEMANY, LLC, YOSEF TAHBAZOF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication is Granted in Favor of all Three Moving Defendants. All three moving defendants satisfied their burden of presenting sufficient admissible evidence showing that they are not liable to plaintiff Rodrigo Lopez on any of Mr. Lopez's nine causes of action. Mr. Lopez failed to present any admissible evidence creating a triable dispute on any of his claims against any of the moving defendants. The undisputed material facts establish that none of the moving defendants expressly agreed to or acquiesced in Mr. Lopez residing in the building and thus the moving defendants did not have any contractual, statutory or common law duty to maintain the portion of the building where Mr. Lopez resided in a condition fit for Mr. Lopez's habitation. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD PARK ET AL VS. MAGID ABRAHAM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Ccp Section 877 6 A2 And Request To Dismiss Cross Actions Pursuant To Cal Rules Of Court Rule 3 1382
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 8, 2021 line . CROSS DEFENDANT NEAL WARD, MAGDALENA VISSER, COMPASS SF I INC., F/K/A PARAGON REAL ESTATE GROUP, COMPASS CALIFORNIA II INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Ccp Section 877 6 A2 And Request To Dismiss Cross Actions Pursuant To Cal Rules Of Court Rule 3 1382 is Denied. Regardless of whether the total claimed legally allowable damages of plaintiffs Howard and June Park are closer to $6 million or closer to $2 million, cross-complainants Magid and Linda Abraham satisfied their burden of presenting admissible evidence that $100,000 is far too low to be a fair approximation for the proportional responsibility of the Compass cross-defendants and thus is far outside the ballpark. The correspondence between the Abrahams and Compass shows that the Abrahams reasonably relied on Compass to assist the Abrahams with their disclosures and Compass agreed to provide such assistance. The declarations of the Abrahams show that Mr. Ward knew about the Abrahams' recent purchase of the house and their even more recent unsuccessful efforts to sell the house yet Mr. Ward, a self-described elite broker for the sale of high-end houses, did not ask the Abrahams for all the reports relating to the Abrahams' purchase and unsuccessful sale of the house. Nor does Mr. Ward say that he made such a request in his declaration. This failure appears to be a breach of the standard of care, as shown by page 125 of Ms. Hatvany's deposition, and also appears to be causally related to most or all of the Parks' claimed damages. Consequently, the Compass cross-defendants' settlement with the Parks cannot be justified by the no liability position taken in the moving papers or proportionality based on the commission percentage position asserted in the reply papers. The indemnity and general disclosure duties language in the Abrahams/Compass listing agreement does not preclude the Compass cross-defendants' liability for equitable indemnity based on failure of the Compass cross-defendants to satisfy their standard of care. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC VS. ALAN KINGSTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 8, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT ALLAN KINGSTON Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories is OFF CALENDAR. The motion is premature in light of the parties' agreement to place a hold on discovery including this discovery dispute and their consequent incomplete meet and confer efforts. As guidance for the parties going forward, if this discovery dispute is revived, the court encourages plaintiff to respond to the interrogatories at issue because those interrogatories are reasonably likely to lead to admissible evidence on defendant's affirmative defense of waiver and similarly encourages defendant to drop his request for sanctions due to the preface to the interrogatories showing that they were intended for a situation not applicable to this case. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
